### Post by rich86 on 2007-06-06
hey, i got a picture simular to that from windows with my nvidia card when either the region settings were wrong eg PAL and ...NSTC (is that the right one).  or the adaptor output type was wrong (eg s-video, scart or composite)
not sure how to change settings in ubuntu though

---

### Post by dfreer on 2007-06-06
have you tried running sudo nvidia-settings, and configuring the tv-out there? backup your old /etc/X11/xorg.conf file, run that command, configure everything, and then have it save all of the settings to /etc/X11/xorg.conf. Reboot. if it works, great. if not, restore the backup from the CLI and you could try again with a different setting.

p.s. NTSC should work if you are in the USA, anywhere else and you'll have to figure out what type you need to use.

---

### Post by drspastic on 2007-06-06
Thanks rich86 and dfreer

Yeah I've used the nvidia settings but cant use it to change refresh rate - which is what I believe is causing the problem.

I'm using SVIDEO and get output but I do get the lines.

What I don't understand is why I get the lines when running my installed Ubuntu with the driver installed, but no lines when running off the liveCD with no driver!

Thanks

---

### Post by dfreer on 2007-06-06
if you can get it to work with the Live CD, try copying your /etc/X11/xorg.conf from the live cd to replace your installed system's /etc/X11/xorg.conf. Also check and make sure that the live CD is using the same video driver.

---
